description: Systemctl is the primary command-line utility for managing systemd services, targets, and global Linux init system processes.

commands.explanation: Outputs the running status, diagnostic logs, and absolute state of a specific service.
Immediately boots up and starts the specified service daemon for the current session.
Gracefully halts the active running service.
Stops and then restarts a service. Disconnects existing client connections.
Reloads the service's configuration files without terminating the core process or dropping active connections.
Configures the service to persistently automatically start every time the server boots.
Removes the symlinks, preventing the service from starting automatically upon reboot.
Completely prevents the service from being started on the system, manually or automatically, by mapping it to /dev/null.
Provides a direct printout of any services that crashed or failed to load correctly.
Forces systemd to reload all .service unit files. Crucial after you edit a custom service configuration script.
